name: Mzansi Mix Vol. 3
description: Logic and reasoning with a South African twist — nature, food, travel, music, and tech puzzles
author: Game Night Crew

questions.question: How many strings does a standard guitar have?
questions.explanation: A standard guitar has 6 strings. Whether you're playing maskandi or rock, it's always 6!

questions.question: You bake 24 koeksisters and eat 3 yourself. How many are left to share?
questions.explanation: 24 minus 3 = 21 koeksisters left. Shame, try not to eat them all before the guests arrive!

questions.question: Which is bigger: Table Mountain or a table?
questions.explanation: Table Mountain is over 1,000 metres high. Even the biggest dining table can't compete with that! Don't overthink this one.

questions.question: A recipe needs 3 eggs to make 12 muffins. How many eggs do you need for 12 muffins?
questions.explanation: The answer is right there in the question — 3 eggs for 12 muffins. Don't let your brain make it harder than it is!

questions.question: If you drive from Johannesburg heading south, which ocean would you eventually reach?
questions.explanation: Heading south from Joburg takes you towards the Indian Ocean coast (think Durban way). Lekker beach vibes!

questions.question: A safari guide spots 4 elephants, each with 3 birds on their backs. How many legs are there in total (elephants + birds)?
questions.explanation: 4 elephants x 4 legs = 16 legs. 4 x 3 = 12 birds x 2 legs = 24 legs. Total: 16 + 24 = 40 legs. The bush is full of legs!

questions.question: Mama's melktert recipe serves 8. She needs to make it for 20 people. If the recipe calls for 400ml of milk, how much milk does she need?
questions.explanation: She needs to multiply by 20/8 = 2.5. So 400ml x 2.5 = 1,000ml (1 litre). That's one big melktert!

questions.question: A playlist has 15 amapiano songs averaging 3 minutes each and 5 house songs averaging 6 minutes each. What's the total playlist length in minutes?
questions.explanation: 15 x 3 = 45 minutes of amapiano + 5 x 6 = 30 minutes of house = 75 minutes total. Lekker vibes for over an hour!

questions.question: If a chameleon changes colour every 5 minutes and you watch it for an hour, how many colour changes do you see?
questions.explanation: 60 minutes / 5 minutes = 12 colour changes. Our little friend is putting on quite the fashion show!

questions.question: Durban is approximately 600 km from Joburg. If you drive at 120 km/h, how long is the trip?
questions.explanation: 600 / 120 = 5 hours. That's assuming no stops for a Wimpy pie on the way! Distance = speed x time, simple maths.

questions.question: A Kruger Park ranger counts Big 5 sightings in a week: 12 elephants, 5 lions, 3 leopards, 8 buffalo, and 2 rhinos. What percentage of sightings were lions?
questions.explanation: Total sightings: 12+5+3+8+2 = 30. Lions: 5/30 = 1/6 = 16.7%. Not bad for a week in the bush!

questions.question: A biltong maker buys 10 kg of beef at R180/kg and sells the dried biltong (which weighs 40% of the original) at R600/kg. What's his profit in Rand?
questions.explanation: Cost: 10 x R180 = R1,800. Biltong weight: 10 x 0.4 = 4 kg. Revenue: 4 x R600 = R2,400. Profit: R2,400 - R1,800 = R600. The biltong game pays!

questions.question: An amapiano DJ plays a set with a pattern: 2 fast songs, then 1 slow song, repeating. If the set has 21 songs, how many slow songs are there?
questions.explanation: Each cycle is 3 songs (2 fast + 1 slow). 21 / 3 = 7 complete cycles = 7 slow songs. That's the rhythm of the night!

questions.question: A Cape Town wine farm produces 500 bottles per barrel. If they have 12 barrels and sell each bottle for R85, but 5% of bottles are rejected in quality control, how much revenue do they make?
questions.explanation: Total bottles: 12 x 500 = 6,000. After 5% rejected: 6,000 x 0.95 = 5,700 bottles. Revenue: 5,700 x R85 = R484,500. Cheers to that!

questions.question: Put these animals in order from SMALLEST to LARGEST: (1) African elephant, (2) Meerkat, (3) Springbok, (4) Hippopotamus
questions.explanation: Meerkat (~730g), Springbok (~40kg), Hippo (~1,500kg), African Elephant (~6,000kg). From tiny to mighty in the African bush!

questions.question: Your phone battery drains 12% per hour when using GPS. You start a road trip at 100% at 6am. At what time will your phone die?
questions.explanation: 100% / 12% per hour = 8.33 hours. 8 hours 20 minutes after 6:00am = 2:20pm. Better bring a car charger, boet!

questions.question: A Nando's orders 250 chickens on Monday, 180 on Wednesday, and 320 on Friday. If each chicken weighs 1.5 kg and costs R45 per kg, what's the weekly cost in Rand?
questions.explanation: Total chickens: 250 + 180 + 320 = 750. Weight: 750 x 1.5 = 1,125 kg. Cost: 1,125 x R45 = R50,625. That's a LOT of peri-peri!

questions.question: A music festival has 3 stages. Stage A plays every 30 min, Stage B every 45 min, and Stage C every 60 min. If they all start at noon, when do all three stages next play at the same time?
questions.explanation: Find the LCM of 30, 45, and 60. LCM = 180 minutes = 3 hours. So they all sync again at 3:00pm. Time to catch all three acts!

questions.question: A safari lodge charges R2,500 per person per night. A family of 4 stays for 3 nights. Children under 12 get 50% off. If 2 of the family are children under 12, what's the total cost?
questions.explanation: 2 adults: 2 x R2,500 x 3 = R15,000. 2 children at 50%: 2 x R1,250 x 3 = R7,500. Total: R15,000 + R7,500 = R22,500. Safari is not cheap, but it's worth every cent!

questions.question: A Durban bunny chow shop sells 3 sizes: quarter (R55), half (R85), and full (R120). On a busy day they sell 40 quarters, 25 halves and 15 fulls. What's the total revenue in Rand?
questions.explanation: 40 x R55 = R2,200. 25 x R85 = R2,125. 15 x R120 = R1,800. Total: R2,200 + R2,125 + R1,800 = R6,125. That's a lekker day in the bunny business!

questions.question: On a hike up Lion's Head, you climb 200m in the first hour, 150m in the second, and 100m in the third (you're getting tired). If this pattern continues, what's the maximum height you can reach?
questions.explanation: The pattern decreases by 50m each hour: 200, 150, 100, 50, then 0. Total: 200+150+100+50 = 500m. After that you're too tired to climb! That's your max.

questions.question: A rooibos tea farmer plants trees in a triangular pattern: 1 in row 1, 2 in row 2, 3 in row 3, etc. How many trees are there in a plantation with 12 rows?
questions.explanation: Sum of 1 to 12 = 12 x 13 / 2 = 78 trees. The formula n(n+1)/2 is your friend here. That's a lot of rooibos!

questions.question: You're making potjiekos and the recipe says simmer at a temperature that's 40% of the way between 80°C and 180°C. What temperature is that?
questions.explanation: Range = 180 - 80 = 100°C. 40% of 100 = 40°C. So 80 + 40 = 120°C. Lekker, now keep that potjie at a steady simmer!

questions.question: A WiFi signal loses 50% of its strength with each wall it passes through. After passing through 3 walls, what percentage of the original signal remains?
questions.explanation: After wall 1: 50%. After wall 2: 25%. After wall 3: 12.5%. Each wall halves what's left. No wonder the WiFi is so slow in the back room!

questions.question: Put these distances in order from SHORTEST to LONGEST: (1) Joburg to Pretoria (~58km), (2) Cape Town to Stellenbosch (~50km), (3) Durban to Pietermaritzburg (~80km), (4) Bloemfontein to Maseru (~140km)
questions.explanation: Cape Town-Stellenbosch (~50km), Joburg-Pretoria (~58km), Durban-PMB (~80km), Bloem-Maseru (~140km). Know your SA distances, road tripper!

questions.question: A food truck at a market sells gatsby sandwiches. It costs R35 to make each one and they sell for R80. Fixed costs are R900 per market day. How many gatsbys must they sell to break even?
questions.explanation: Profit per gatsby: R80 - R35 = R45. Break even: R900 / R45 = 20 gatsbys. After 20, you're making money. Hustle hard!

questions.question: A nature reserve has a rectangular dam that's 60m x 40m with a depth of 2m. How many litres of water does it hold when full? (1 cubic metre = 1,000 litres). Give answer in thousands.
questions.explanation: Volume: 60 x 40 x 2 = 4,800 cubic metres. In litres: 4,800 x 1,000 = 4,800,000 litres. Answer in thousands = 4,800. That's a big dam, literally!

questions.question: A DJ charges R2,000 for the first 3 hours and R500 for each additional hour. A year-end party runs from 7pm to 2am. How much does the DJ cost?
questions.explanation: 7pm to 2am = 7 hours. First 3 hours: R2,000. Remaining 4 hours: 4 x R500 = R2,000. Total: R4,000. Music is life, but it costs!

questions.question: You buy data bundles: 1GB for R99 or 3GB for R249. You need 9GB for the month. How much do you save by buying the bigger bundles?
questions.explanation: Buying 1GB bundles: 9 x R99 = R891. Buying 3GB bundles: 3 x R249 = R747. Savings: R891 - R747 = R144. Buy smart, save more data money!

questions.question: A Karoo farm has a windmill that pumps 200 litres of water per hour. The farm needs 3,600 litres per day. How many hours per day must the windmill run?
questions.explanation: 3,600 / 200 = 18 hours of pumping. That windmill better be reliable — the sheep are thirsty!

questions.question: You're at a wine tasting with 6 different wines. How many unique pairs of wines can you compare by tasting two at a time?
questions.explanation: Combinations: C(6,2) = 6! / (2! x 4!) = 15 pairs. That's a lot of sipping! Use the formula n(n-1)/2 = 6x5/2 = 15.

questions.question: A hiking group walks at 4 km/h uphill and 6 km/h downhill. If the trail is 12 km up and 12 km down, how many total hours does the round trip take?
questions.explanation: Uphill: 12/4 = 3 hours. Downhill: 12/6 = 2 hours. Total: 5 hours. Pack enough water and biltong for the trip!

questions.question: Three friends order from a food truck: a gatsby (R80), a bunny chow (R65), and a vetkoek (R30). They split the total equally. How much does each person owe?
questions.explanation: Total: R80 + R65 + R30 = R175. Split 3 ways: R175/3 = R58.33 each. Hope someone has coins for the change!

questions.question: A wildlife photographer takes 500 photos on safari. She deletes 60% as blurry. Of the remaining, she edits 75% for her portfolio. How many edited photos does she have?
questions.explanation: After deleting 60%: 500 x 0.40 = 200 clear photos. Edits 75% of those: 200 x 0.75 = 150 portfolio-ready shots. Quality over quantity!

questions.question: Put these SA music genres in order from OLDEST origin to NEWEST: (1) Amapiano, (2) Kwaito, (3) Mbaqanga, (4) Gqom
questions.explanation: Mbaqanga (1960s), Kwaito (1990s), Gqom (2010s), Amapiano (late 2010s). SA music keeps evolving, and we love it!

questions.question: A Table Mountain cableway car holds 65 people and takes 5 minutes up, 2 minutes to load, 5 minutes down, 2 minutes to load. With one car running, how many people can go up in one hour?
questions.explanation: Full cycle: 5 + 2 + 5 + 2 = 14 minutes. In 60 minutes: 60/14 = 4.28 cycles, so 4 complete cycles. But wait — the car can take people UP more cleverly. Actually, one full round trip = 14 min. In 60 min that's 4 complete trips (56 min). 4 x 65 = 260 people up the mountain!

questions.question: A protea grows 2cm in its first year, and each subsequent year it grows 1.5 times the previous year's growth. How many centimetres tall is it after 5 years? Round to the nearest whole number.
questions.explanation: Year 1: 2cm. Year 2: 3cm. Year 3: 4.5cm. Year 4: 6.75cm. Year 5: 10.125cm. Total height: 2 + 3 + 4.5 + 6.75 + 10.125 = 26.375, rounds to 26cm. Geometric growth is lekker powerful!

questions.question: You have a 10-digit SA phone number (starting with 0). How many unique phone numbers are possible if the second digit must be 6, 7, or 8 (mobile prefixes)?
questions.explanation: First digit is fixed (0). Second digit: 3 choices. Remaining 8 digits: 10 choices each = 10^8 = 100,000,000. Total: 3 x 100,000,000 = 300 million possible numbers. That's a lot of phone numbers for Mzansi!

questions.question: On a road trip, you notice your car's fuel efficiency drops by 20% when the aircon is on. Normally you get 12 km/L. If you drive 300 km with aircon in 35°C Durban heat, how many extra litres of fuel do you use compared to no aircon?
questions.explanation: Normal: 300/12 = 25 litres. With aircon (80% efficiency = 9.6 km/L): 300/9.6 = 31.25 litres. Extra: 31.25 - 25 = 6.25 litres. But in that heat, you NEED the aircon!

questions.question: A tech startup in Cape Town has 5 developers. Each developer reviews every other developer's code once per sprint. Each review takes 30 minutes. How many total hours are spent on code reviews per sprint?
questions.explanation: Each of 5 devs reviews 4 others = 20 reviews total. 20 x 30 min = 600 minutes = 10 hours. That's a lot of PR reviews, but quality code matters!

questions.question: A circular lapa (outdoor area) has a diameter of 14 metres. If you want to put fairy lights around the entire circumference, how many metres of lights do you need? Use pi = 22/7. Give your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: Circumference = pi x diameter = 22/7 x 14 = 44 metres exactly. That lapa is going to look stunning for the jol!

questions.question: A Karoo farmer has a water tank that loses 2% of its water each day to evaporation. Starting with 10,000 litres, approximately how many litres remain after 10 days?
questions.explanation: It's compound: 10,000 x (0.98)^10 = 10,000 x 0.8171 = 8,171 litres. Not 80% because the 2% applies to a decreasing amount each day! Compound effects are sneaky.

questions.question: You have a 4-digit PIN for your banking app. If no digit can be repeated, and the first digit can't be 0, how many possible PINs are there?
questions.explanation: First digit: 9 choices (1-9). Second: 9 remaining (0-9 minus the first). Third: 8. Fourth: 7. Total: 9 x 9 x 8 x 7 = 4,536. Keep that PIN safe, boet!

questions.question: A sundial in a Stellenbosch garden shows 2pm. If you look at it in a mirror, what time does it appear to show?
questions.explanation: A mirror reverses the image. On a clock face, 2 o'clock reflected becomes 10 o'clock. The mirror flips left-right, so the hands appear at the mirror position. Clever, ne?

questions.question: Put these SA creatures in order of MOST to FEWEST legs: (1) Shongololo (millipede), (2) Crab, (3) Spider, (4) Beetle
questions.explanation: Shongololo (hundreds of legs!), Crab (10 legs), Spider (8 legs), Beetle (6 legs). From creepy-crawly champion to six-legged beetle. Know your critters!

questions.question: A Fibonacci-loving chef creates a tasting menu where each course has a number of bites equal to the Fibonacci sequence (1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13...). If there are 8 courses, how many bites in total?
questions.explanation: Fibonacci: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21. Sum: 1+1+2+3+5+8+13+21 = 54 bites total. That's one mathematical feast!

questions.question: In a photo, 5 friends stand in a row. How many different arrangements are possible if the two tallest friends refuse to stand next to each other?
questions.explanation: Total arrangements: 5! = 120. Arrangements where the two ARE together: treat them as one unit = 4! x 2 = 48. So arrangements where they're NOT together: 120 - 48 = 72. Complementary counting for the win!

questions.question: You're lost in the Cederberg mountains with no compass. At noon, the sun is roughly north (Southern Hemisphere). Your shadow points south. If you face your shadow, which direction is to your LEFT?
questions.explanation: In the Southern Hemisphere at noon, the sun is roughly north. Facing your shadow (south), east is to your left. This is the opposite of the Northern Hemisphere! Knowing this could save your life in the veld.

questions.question: A password must be 6 characters: first 2 are uppercase letters, next 2 are digits, last 2 are lowercase letters. No character can repeat within its category. How many possible passwords are there? Express your answer in millions, rounded to the nearest million.
questions.explanation: Uppercase: 26 x 25 = 650. Digits: 10 x 9 = 90. Lowercase: 26 x 25 = 650. Total: 650 x 90 x 650 = 38,025,000. Rounded = 38 million possible passwords. Good luck guessing that!

questions.question: An ant starts at corner A of a cube and wants to reach the diagonally opposite corner B by walking along edges only. What's the MINIMUM number of edges it must traverse?
questions.explanation: To get from one corner of a cube to the diagonally opposite corner along edges, you need exactly 3 edges — one in each dimension (x, y, z). Think of it as going right, up, then forward. No shortcuts on a cube!

questions.question: A perfect number equals the sum of its proper divisors (excluding itself). 6 is perfect (1+2+3=6). What is the next perfect number after 6?
questions.explanation: 28's divisors: 1, 2, 4, 7, 14. Sum: 1+2+4+7+14 = 28. Perfect! The next ones are 496 and 8128. These numbers are incredibly rare — only 51 are known to exist. Maths is beautiful, ne?

questions.question: A winemaker blends 3 wines. Blend A is 60% Cabernet, 40% Merlot. Blend B is 80% Cabernet, 20% Shiraz. She mixes A and B in a 2:3 ratio. What percentage of the final blend is Cabernet?
questions.explanation: In the 2:3 mix: Cabernet from A: 2/5 x 60% = 24%. Cabernet from B: 3/5 x 80% = 48%. Total Cabernet: 24% + 48% = 72%. That's a bold Cabernet-dominant blend!

questions.question: You have 5 different spices and want to create unique curry blends using AT LEAST 2 spices. How many different blends can you make?
questions.explanation: Total subsets of 5 items: 2^5 = 32. Minus the empty set (1) and single-spice sets (5) = 32 - 6 = 26 unique blends. Combinatorics meets the kitchen!

questions.question: In the word STELLENBOSCH, how many distinct ways can you arrange all the letters? S(2), T(1), E(2), L(2), N(1), B(1), O(1), C(1), H(1). Give your answer as a number.
questions.explanation: STELLENBOSCH has 12 letters with repeats: S(2), E(2), L(2). Arrangements: 12! / (2! x 2! x 2!) = 479,001,600 / 8 = 59,875,200. Eish, that's a lot of anagrams for one wine town!

questions.question: Rank these SA national parks by size from SMALLEST to LARGEST: (1) Kruger National Park, (2) Table Mountain National Park, (3) Kgalagadi Transfrontier Park, (4) Addo Elephant National Park
questions.explanation: Table Mountain NP (~221 km²), Addo Elephant NP (~1,640 km²), Kruger NP (~19,485 km²), Kgalagadi Transfrontier (~38,000 km² total). The Kgalagadi is massive — it spans SA and Botswana! Only a true nature lover gets this right.
